displayName: Adjudication Gate (engine-decided auto-clear vs human review)
description: Engine-evaluated gate on the CRA's rendered adjudication. The criteria carry the operator's auto-clear policy (autoClearOutcome, the single engineOutcome value that proceeds to hire with NO human review — for employment this is 'pass'/MEETS REQUIREMENTS); the performer submits the observedOutcome the CRA returned (ironvale-consumer-report-v1.engineOutcome) as completion evidence. The rules engine settles FULFILLED when observedOutcome == autoClearOutcome (auto-clear, hire proceeds) and FAILED otherwise (needs-review or fail -> a human MUST run an individualized assessment via ironvale-individualized-assessment-v1). The ENGINE decides review-required from the CRA's rendered outcome; this deliberately replaces an agent-asserted `adverseCapable`/`needsReview` boolean an agent could set false-ly to launder an adverse-capable report past human review (the F-758 self-attested-control anti-pattern). | autoClearOutcome is OPERATOR POLICY: the employer configures which outcome clears without review and must keep that policy documented. On FAILED, open an ironvale-individualized-assessment-v1 and set gateRef to THIS record id to route the candidate to a human reviewer. | The gate uses the engine's string:exact verb (case-sensitive equality) on the normalized engineOutcome; string verbs have no widening tolerance, so there is no F-787-style band-widening dodge here.

reportRef.description: recordId of the ironvale-consumer-report-v1 whose outcome is being banded.

autoClearOutcome.description: The engineOutcome that auto-clears with no human review (operator policy). Single-valued for employment: only a clean 'pass' proceeds without an individualized assessment; needs-review and fail both require a human.

observedOutcome.description: The engineOutcome the CRA returned for this candidate (ironvale-consumer-report-v1.engineOutcome).
